channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Can you build observability ingestion on S3 alone — no Kafka, no disks, no coordination layer?
item.description: TL;DR — A Kafka + Flink + OTel ingestion pipeline cost us ~$700–800/month at 10 MB/s. We rebuilt it as a single binary where the data, the write-ahead log, and the Iceberg catalog all live in S3...

item.title: How to Learn System Design From Scratch (With No Distributed Systems Experience)
item.description: If you have ever opened a system design article, seen a diagram with twelve boxes, three databases, a message queue, and the words "eventually consistent," and quietly closed the tab, this post is...

item.title: AGENTS.md Is Not Enough for Safe AI Agent Execution
item.description: Overview AGENTS.md is useful. It gives AI coding agents a place to find repo-specific guidance: how to behave what conventions matter what areas need extra caution what kinds of changes should...

item.title: Learn DynamoDB by running it - accesspatterns.dev
item.description: I've been building on DynamoDB since around 2015, and these days I build tools for it: dynoxide, a DynamoDB engine, and Nubo, a native client. So I'm not neutral about it. It's the first database...

item.title: How I Built an AI Tattoo Design Generator (and What I Learned)
item.description: A few months ago, I set out to solve a problem I kept hearing about in tattoo communities: people struggle to visualize their tattoo ideas before committing to the chair. The result is AI Tattoo...

item.title: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission fined Amazon $2.25 million to settle claims that the company failed to help customers who fell victim to identity theft, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. In its...

item.title: Passwords are not necessary, a Oauth/OIDC approach
item.description: Intro It is possible to skip the burden of password management, security, encryption and password rotations. When using OAuth 2.0 with OIDC (OpenID Connect), the user can log in with third-party...

item.title: Someone asked me if I knew of an alternative project management tool, so I developed one in about 24 hours.
item.description: AI is transforming the way we develop software. In the past, creating specific software required an entire team and months of work; AI has accelerated this process a hundredfold—the speed at which...

item.title: Why Bluetooth Is Named After a Viking King
item.description: Pick up almost any connected gadget today and you will find it somewhere: a pair of wireless earbuds, a fitness band, a smart speaker, a car stereo. The little angular logo and the word...

item.title: How to Change a Public Repository to Private Using GitHub CLI
item.description: When working on personal, academic, or professional projects, we often start a repository as public to share progress, test configurations, or collaborate openly. However, at some point, it may...

item.title: Route inbound mail to the right agent automatically
item.description: Most "AI email agent" demos run one model against one mailbox: every message that lands gets the same prompt, the same context, the same handler. That's fine for a toy. It falls apart the moment...

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
item.description: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.

item.title: Acer’s Swift Go 16 is a lot of laptop for $900
item.description: As high memory and storage prices have driven up the cost of everything from consoles to computers, finding a competent laptop for under $1,000 has become a challenge. Thankfully, the Acer Swift...

item.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
item.description: Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate...

item.title: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.description: Google is updating its image generator to make it faster and cheaper, making it a more useful tool for creators looking to make AI content.

item.title: LG’s 27-inch Tandem OLED gaming monitor is cheaper than ever
item.description: The price of LG’s UltraGear 27GX700A-B 1440p gaming monitor that came out last August has dropped to nearly its best price yet. You can grab it at Amazon for $484.99 or at LG for $499.99...

item.title: ScarfBench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Enterprise Java Framework Migration
007 First Light’s developer lays off staff but claims its next franchise will continue
item.description: IO Interactive, the studio behind the Hitman series and 007 First Light, announced that it is laying off staff after a relationship with an "external partner" on its next big franchise, Project...

item.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
item.description: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using...

item.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
item.description: New Microsoft research shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to...

item.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
item.description: A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and...

item.title: Moto’s Tag 2 tracker is $20 for a limited time
item.description: Announced at CES 2026, the Moto Tag 2 has finally arrived in the US. The Bluetooth tracker with improved battery life over its predecessor, plus UWB sensing for more accurate tracking, is...

item.title: Roundtables: Longevity’s Next Frontier: “Reprogramming” Your Body
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Billions of dollars are flooding into efforts to reverse aging as scientists explore ways to return cells to a younger state. But how far off are these...

item.title: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
item.description: A new teaser trailer confirmed that Wonka's The Golden Ticket will premiere on Netflix on September 23rd, following its Squid Game reality show in the trend of creating real competitions based on...

item.title: Rockstar workers push to unionize ahead of GTA VI’s launch
item.description: Workers at Grand Theft Auto VI developer Rockstar Games have submitted a request for their union, the IWGB Game Workers Union, to be voluntarily recognized, according to a press release. The...

item.title: Google’s killing off Tenor GIF searches in other apps
item.description: The GIF-picking interfaces in some of your favorite online platforms might look different going forward, as Google prepares to shut down the Tenor API today. While the Tenor website, along with...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.
